summary: A square-walled courtyard building with four tall corner towers and a large semicircular stepped red-tile roof rising from one side into the center. The main enclosure is three stories tall with horizontal white banding and regular small windows; towers are tall rectangular pillars topped with wide overhanging layered roofs. The structure sits on a flat cleared plot with a low white base course around the interior footprint.

shape.overall_form: square outer wall forming a courtyard with four corner towers and an internal semicircular roofed hall attached to one side
shape.symmetry: rough fourfold symmetry around courtyard with asymmetry introduced by the semicircular roofed hall on one side

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: tower trim / corner pillars / roof edging

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: horizontal banding / base blocks / decorative slabs

elements: four tall corner towers with overhanging layered caps
three-storey outer walls with regular small windows
semicircular stepped brick roof/hall protruding into courtyard
continuous white base course/banding around walls
open central courtyard (ground level appears empty)
rebuild_hints: Lay out a square outer perimeter (~28x28) with a 1-block-thick exterior wall and a 1-block-wide internal white base course visible around the courtyard.
Place four rectangular towers at the corners, each about 3-4 blocks thick and ~14-16 blocks tall, topped by a 5x5 overhanging layered cap using stone/stone slabs and a single light block at center.
Construct the main enclosure as three stacked stories with horizontal white banding (quartz) between floors, add regularly spaced 1x1 windows (glass) on each level.

Build the semicircular internal hall on one side using layered brick stairs/blocks to form a stepped half-dome roof rising from the courtyard floor into the center; align its flat face with the outer wall.
Use sandstone (or similar pale block) for majority of walls, quartz for trim and base, brick for curved roof; add small stone/ slab details on tower corners and caps.
uncertainties: Exact block types: pale walls look like sandstone but could be a different pale block.
Precise footprint dimensions and tower thickness are estimated from images and may vary by a few blocks.
Interior layout of floors and any internal supports is not visible; courtyard ground use unknown.
